blog.headline: Field notes from the car line
blog.lede: Practical writing for school front offices, principals, and district IT — on dismissal, parent communication, safety, and the messy middle of rolling a system across multiple campuses.
blog.metaDescriptionEmpty: PickupRoster blog — field notes on dismissal, car lines, and district rollouts.
blog.metaDescriptionIndex: Field notes on school dismissal, car-line operations, and district rollouts from the PickupRoster team.
blog.ogDescription: Field notes on school dismissal, car-line operations, and district rollouts.

faqs.heading: FAQs

domain.a: Yes — custom domains (or a subdomain on your school's own domain) are available on Campus and District plans. Point DNS to the same Worker as your other hosts. Car Line uses a yourslug.pickuproster.com subdomain.
domain.q: Can we use our own domain?

trial.a: Your trial ends on the later of 30 calendar days from signup or 25 qualifying pickup days. A qualifying day is a day with more than 10 distinct students marked as called.

viewer.a: Share the viewer PIN or a magic link. Viewer sessions are scoped to your organization and respect lockouts after failed attempts.
viewer.q: How do families view the board?

where.a: Each organization gets its own subdomain like yourslug.pickuproster.com. The main domain shows this marketing site; the board never mixes tenants. On Campus and District plans you can instead use a custom domain or a subdomain on your school's own domain (for example, pickup.yourschool.edu).

faqs.lede: Straight answers for admins and IT.
faqs.metaDescription: Common questions about car line boards, trials, and security.

guides.headline: How to set up and run PickupRoster
guides.lede: Step-by-step guides for school administrators — from importing your first roster to running a fire drill from your phone.
guides.metaDescriptionEmpty: PickupRoster guides — how-to articles for school administrators.
guides.metaDescriptionIndex: How-to guides for setting up and running PickupRoster — roster imports, drill templates, branding, billing, and dismissal-day operations.
guides.ogDescription: How-to guides for setting up and running PickupRoster across your school or district.

marketing.faqLanguageAnswerSuffix: If your school needs a language we don't yet ship, contact support — we add languages based on customer demand.

features.liveBoard: Live board with WebSocket updates and viewer PINs or magic links.
features.subdomain: Subdomain per school (yourslug.pickuproster.com); Campus and District plans can bring a custom domain or a subdomain on your school's own domain.
features.trial: Trial that respects real usage: calendar time and qualifying pickup days.

footerCta.body: Start a trial from the marketing site. Your live board lives on your school subdomain—never mixed with other tenants.
footerCta.heading: Ready when your school is

marketingLanding.headline: One board for pickups, drills, and daily chaos—without the walkie chatter.
marketingLanding.lede: Pickup Roster gives each school its own live board, viewer access families trust, and admin tools your front office already understands.

midTrialCheckin.para1: You're about halfway through your trial. Just wanted to check in — not to sell you anything, just to see how it's going.
midTrialCheckin.para2: If anything's confusing, awkward, or missing, hit reply and tell me. I read every email, and I'd rather hear about it now than not at all.
midTrialCheckin.para3: If you'd like to keep poking around, you can jump back in here: {{appLink}}
midTrialCheckin.para3Html: If you'd like to keep poking around, you can jump back in here: {{appLink}}
midTrialCheckin.preview: Just checking in — no agenda, just want to make sure it's useful.
midTrialCheckin.subject: how's pickup going, {{firstName}}?

passwordReset.didNotRequest: If you didn't request this, you can ignore this email — your password won't change{{requestIpSuffix}}.
passwordReset.fallbackPrefix: Or paste this link into your browser:

passwordReset.preview: Use this link to choose a new password.

trialCta.body: 30-day free trial. No credit card required. Built for schools running dismissal for 300 to 3,000 students.
trialCta.headline: Get structured dismissal up and running in a week.

one.para1: Your trial ends tomorrow. To keep PickupRoster running for {{orgName}} without interruption, upgrade here: {{appLink}}
one.para1Html: Your trial ends tomorrow. To keep PickupRoster running for {{orgName}} without interruption, upgrade here: {{appLink}}
one.preview: one click to keep PickupRoster running for {{orgName}}.
one.subject: your trial ends tomorrow

seven.para1: Quick heads-up: your trial ends on {{trialEndDate}}, about a week from now.
seven.para2: If pickup at {{orgName}} has felt a little less chaotic lately, that's the part worth keeping. Upgrading now means no interruption, your roster stays put, and tomorrow's dismissal runs exactly like today's.
seven.para3: You can upgrade in a couple of clicks here: {{appLink}}
seven.para3Html: You can upgrade in a couple of clicks here: {{appLink}}
seven.para4: Questions? Just reply — I'll get back to you.
seven.preview: {{daysLeft}} days to keep your setup — here's how.
seven.subject: one week left on your PickupRoster trial

three.para1: Your trial ends on {{trialEndDate}} — just 3 days away.
three.para2: I don't want you to lose the setup you've built for {{orgName}} or have next week's dismissal line go back to the way it used to be. Upgrading takes a minute and everything keeps working.
three.para4: If something's giving you pause, tell me what it is. I want to know.
three.preview: I don't want you to lose your setup at {{orgName}}.
three.subject: 3 days left, {{firstName}}

welcome.para1: Thanks for signing up. Helping schools have a calmer pickup is the reason I started PickupRoster, so it really means a lot that {{orgName}} gave it a try.
welcome.para2: I'd love to hear what dismissal looks like at your school today — what's working, what isn't, and what you wish someone would fix. Could we grab 15 minutes on the phone in the next week or two?
welcome.para3: Just reply with a couple of times that work for you and I'll send over an invite.
welcome.para4: Either way, glad to have you here.
welcome.preview: a quick note from the founder — and a small ask.
welcome.subject: thanks for trying PickupRoster, {{firstName}}
